title: Artificial intelligence redefines work and research between optimism and concern
summary: Studies reveal that AI reduces the quality of knowledge in online communities, but also boosts scientific careers, while executives debate its impact on employment.

title: Meta and Apple face lawsuits over privacy and social media addiction
summary: Meta appeals a verdict that blames it for addiction and removes its AI image feature, while Apple sues OpenAI for theft of trade secrets.

title: New gaming hardware launches: AMD, Lenovo, and the DDR5 debate
summary: AMD presents Valorant-themed processors, Lenovo renews its Legion laptop with RTX 5070, and the convenience of DDR5 memory in 2026 is analyzed.

title: LinkedIn leads production of AI-generated content and a YouTuber clones Photoshop in GIMP
summary: A study places LinkedIn as the platform with the most 'AI slop', while a Brazilian creator replicates Photoshop in GIMP and the social network becomes a dating app.

title: Medical advances: shingles vaccine reduces risk of dementia and new sodium battery charges in minutes
summary: The shingles vaccine could prevent dementia, a sodium battery charges in four minutes, and AI generates new peptides for therapeutic purposes.

title: 55% of Americans stopped posting on social media, according to a study
summary: Research reveals that the majority of users abandoned posting status updates on social platforms.

title: Universities ban electronic devices to combat AI in classrooms
summary: The University of Chicago bans electronic devices in law classes, while a Brown professor discovers that grades drop drastically when students do not use AI.

title: Claude Code incorporates web browser and is compared with OpenCode in token usage
summary: The artificial intelligence tool for programming can now read and click on websites, and it is revealed that it sends many more tokens than its competition.

title: Windows 11 Copilot consumes 1 GB of RAM and Windows 95 detected installers by name
summary: The Copilot assistant uses a lot of memory to show PC performance, while a veteran developer explains that Windows 95 did not detect installers but rather guessed.

title: Data centers consume as much electricity as homes in Ireland and cause bacterial contamination
summary: Servers in Ireland use almost the same energy as all homes, and the construction of a Meta data center in Wyoming released rare bacteria.

title: FCC approves orbital mirrors to reflect sunlight and proposes freezing the Arctic
summary: The Federal Communications Commission authorizes satellites with mirrors to illuminate Earth, while a plan suggests refreezing the Arctic to slow melting.

title: Chinese courts allow inheritance of video game accounts and virtual items
summary: Judges set legal precedents that recognize inheritance of digital properties such as games and in-game items.

title: Ukrainians use VPN to alter status of fuel stations on Russian maps
summary: Through VPN connections, Ukrainian citizens modify gas station indicators in map applications to generate chaos in Russia.

title: Sony executives sell shares after announcement of end of physical disc games
summary: Several top Sony executives sold their stakes after PlayStation announced it will stop releasing new games on physical discs.

title: IT administrators show frustration with Windows 11 bloatware
summary: Technology professionals express their discontent with unwanted applications and Microsoft's direction.

title: Linux 7.2-rc3 updates Sega Dreamcast drivers while dropping classic architectures
summary: The new Linux kernel includes fixes for the Dreamcast console, which continues to receive support unlike CPUs such as i486.

title: MSI Afterburner adds frequency/voltage maps for NVIDIA GPUs
summary: The overclocking tool shows how NVIDIA graphics cards follow the Boost curve in real time.

title: AMD tests multi-frame generation FSR up to 8X in its drivers
summary: According to reports, AMD is developing an improved version of FSR that multiplies frames generated by artificial intelligence.

title: Thermaltake launches CAPO X case that houses two gaming PCs in a single chassis
summary: The case allows assembling two independent computers to play on a network, with a price of $186.

title: Sotheby's auctions a functional Apple-1 with serial number 01-0033
summary: The auction house puts up for sale one of the first working Apple computers, part of its science history collection.
